articles.headline: Swift spacecraft reorientation buys time for reboost mission
articles.blurb: NASA modified operations of an astrophysics spacecraft in a decaying orbit to buy more time for a mission later this year that will attempt to raise its orbit.
articles.takeaway: NASA's operational flexibility in spacecraft attitude control demonstrates how ground teams can extend mission life through strategic maneuvering while planning infrastructure upgrades.

articles.headline: Investigators: LaGuardia Controller May Have Stepped Away Before Deadly Crash
articles.blurb: The incident quickly turned tragic.
articles.takeaway: Operational fatigue and inadequate staffing protocols may have created vulnerability gaps in tower coverage during critical traffic handling periods.

articles.headline: 5 Reasons Why Airlines Still Fly The Boeing 747 Freighter
articles.blurb: The Boeing 747 freighter remains valuable for its payload, cargo volume, nose-loading capability, and long-haul performance.
articles.takeaway: The 747F's nose-loading door and 140-ton payload capacity make it irreplaceable for oversize cargo that modern narrowbody freighters cannot accommodate, sustaining its viability despite older design.

articles.headline: How to Assess Safety Risk

articles.headline: Here’s How Much Wider The Airbus A350 Cabin Is Compared To The Boeing 787 Dreamliner

articles.takeaway: The A350's 5-inch cabin width advantage enables airlines to configure 10-abreast seating versus the 787's 9-abreast, creating significant revenue and passenger experience differentiation on long-haul routes.

articles.takeaway: Artemis II's planned 2026 launch represents a critical validation of SLS/Orion systems before crewed lunar landing attempts, with implications for aerospace supply chain and mission-critical component reliability.

articles.headline: Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization
articles.blurb: Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.
articles.takeaway: Operational optimization across all aviation segments can simultaneously address capacity constraints and environmental impacts, with significant research gaps remaining in emerging sustainability technologies.
keywords: aviation; optimization; operations research; sustainability; environmental impacts

articles.headline: Washington Repeals Aircraft Tax Before It Takes Effect

articles.takeaway: Washington's shift from aircraft sales tax to fuel-based taxation redistributes costs from purchasers to operators, potentially affecting fleet purchasing patterns and operating economics across the state.
